mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Negative return frequency remains below 45% when the trigger activates, or the signal loses predictive power during non-recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand, driving sustained positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals fail to produce a positive return frequency above 60% over 60-day horizons, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a disinflationary environment signals healthy capacity utilization without pricing pressure, producing positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses to negative returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 2.5% annually, or Rank IC drops below zero in low-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payroll additions consistently above 150,000 reflect steady economic growth, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns within the following trading week.
failure_condition: Positive return frequency falls below 50% when payroll growth exceeds 150,000, or the signal shows no predictive alpha over a 5-day horizon in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level above 3.0% combined with a flattening yield curve environment triggers valuation compression, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Negative return frequency remains below 40% when the rate threshold is breached, or the signal fails to predict downside risk during tightening phases.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling real personal consumption expenditures indicates corporate profit margin expansion, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces negative returns more than 55% of the time, or the interaction effect vanishes when tested against a baseline single-factor model.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden drop in industrial production below its 12-month moving average during a period of stable employment signals an impending supply shock, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Negative return frequency drops below 45% when the trigger activates, or the signal loses predictive power during periods of high labor market volatility.
